originContent: # Ethereal
translatedContent: # Ethereal

originContent: A custom all-in-one operating system, written for speed, memory conservation, and reliability.\
translatedContent: A custom all-in-one operating system, written for speed, memory conservation, and reliability.\

originContent: Formerly known as reduceOS
translatedContent: Formerly known as reduceOS

originContent: ## What is Ethereal?
translatedContent: ## What is Ethereal?

originContent: Ethereal is a project with the goal of creating a fully functional OS from scratch with all components a modern OS would have.
translatedContent: Ethereal is a project with the goal of creating a fully functional OS from scratch with all components a modern OS would have.

originContent: Currently, the project is developing its usermode stages.
translatedContent: Currently, the project is developing its usermode stages.

originContent: ## Features
translatedContent: ## Features

originContent: - Full SMP-enabled kernel
translatedContent: - Full SMP-enabled kernel

originContent: - Custom window manager (Celestial)
translatedContent: - Custom window manager (Celestial)

originContent: - USB support for UHCI/EHCI/xHCI controllers
translatedContent: - USB support for UHCI/EHCI/xHCI controllers

originContent: - AHCI/IDE support
translatedContent: - AHCI/IDE support

originContent: - Networking stack with E1000 and RTL8139 network card driver
translatedContent: - Networking stack with E1000 and RTL8139 network card driver

originContent: - Priority-based round-robin scheduler with a well-tested API
translatedContent: - Priority-based round-robin scheduler with a well-tested API

originContent: - Support for the `mlibc` C library and its wide variety of functions
translatedContent: - Support for the `mlibc` C library and its wide variety of functions

originContent: - Full ACPI support with the ACPICA library (with backup MinACPI library that doesn't have AML parsing)
translatedContent: - Full ACPI support with the ACPICA library (with backup MinACPI library that doesn't have AML parsing)

originContent: ## Project structure
translatedContent: ## Project structure

originContent: - `base`: Contains the base filesystem. Files in `base/initrd` go in the initial ramdisk (for non LiveCD boots) and files in `base/sysroot` go in sysroot.
translatedContent: - `base`: Contains the base filesystem. Files in `base/initrd` go in the initial ramdisk (for non LiveCD boots) and files in `base/sysroot` go in sysroot.

originContent: - `buildscripts`: Contains buildscripts for the build system
translatedContent: - `buildscripts`: Contains buildscripts for the build system

originContent: - `conf`: Contains misc. configuration files, such as architecture files, GRUB configs, extra boot files, etc.
translatedContent: - `conf`: Contains misc. configuration files, such as architecture files, GRUB configs, extra boot files, etc.

originContent: - `drivers`: Drivers for Hexahedron, copied based on their configuration.
translatedContent: - `drivers`: Drivers for Hexahedron, copied based on their configuration.

originContent: - `external`: Contains external projects, such as ACPICA. See External Components.
translatedContent: - `external`: Contains external projects, such as ACPICA. See External Components.

originContent: - `hexahedron`: The main kernel project
translatedContent: - `hexahedron`: The main kernel project

originContent: - `libkstructures`: Contains misc. kernel structures, like lists/hashmaps/parsers/whatever
translatedContent: - `libkstructures`: Contains misc. kernel structures, like lists/hashmaps/parsers/whatever

originContent: - `libc`: Contains mlibc
translatedContent: - `libc`: Contains mlibc

originContent: ## Building
translatedContent: ## Building

originContent: ### ACPICA notice
translatedContent: ### ACPICA notice

originContent: If you use ACPICA (it is on by default), you must download the tarfile from [here](https://downloadmirror.intel.com/834974/acpica-unix-20240927.tar.gz) and extract it to `external/acpica/acpica-src`
translatedContent: If you use ACPICA (it is on by default), you must download the tarfile from [here](https://downloadmirror.intel.com/834974/acpica-unix-20240927.tar.gz) and extract it to `external/acpica/acpica-src`

originContent: Else, you can edit `conf/build/<arch>.mk` and set `USE_ACPICA` to 0.
translatedContent: Else, you can edit `conf/build/<arch>.mk` and set `USE_ACPICA` to 0.

originContent: ### Building
translatedContent: ### Building

originContent: To build Ethereal, you will need an Ethereal toolchain for your target architecture.\
translatedContent: To build Ethereal, you will need an Ethereal toolchain for your target architecture.\

originContent: The Ethereal toolchain can be found at [the repository](https://github.com/sasdallas/Ethereal-Toolchain)
translatedContent: The Ethereal toolchain can be found at [the repository](https://github.com/sasdallas/Ethereal-Toolchain)

originContent: Other packages required: `grub-common`, `xorriso`, `qemu-system`, `meson`, `ninja`
translatedContent: Other packages required: `grub-common`, `xorriso`, `qemu-system`, `meson`, `ninja`

originContent: Edit `buildscripts/build-arch.sh` to change the target build architecture. \
translatedContent: Edit `buildscripts/build-arch.sh` to change the target build architecture. \

originContent: Running `make all` will build an ISO in `build-output/ethereal.iso`
translatedContent: Running `make all` will build an ISO in `build-output/ethereal.iso`

originContent: Currently, Ethereal's lack of filesystem drivers means that LiveCD boots are usually the best option.\
translatedContent: Currently, Ethereal's lack of filesystem drivers means that LiveCD boots are usually the best option.\

originContent: The initial ramdisk in a LiveCD is the sysroot, and if the OS detects the boot it will copy the initial ramdisk into RAM.
translatedContent: The initial ramdisk in a LiveCD is the sysroot, and if the OS detects the boot it will copy the initial ramdisk into RAM.

originContent: ## Kernel arguments
translatedContent: ## Kernel arguments

originContent: A lot of times, Ethereal fails to load. This is expected. Please start a GitHub issue.
translatedContent: A lot of times, Ethereal fails to load. This is expected. Please start a GitHub issue.

originContent: You can solve some problems by using 'e' to open a GRUB configuration and adding some kernel arguments to the end of the `multiboot entry`.\
translatedContent: You can solve some problems by using 'e' to open a GRUB configuration and adding some kernel arguments to the end of the `multiboot entry`.\

originContent: Here is a small list:
translatedContent: Here is a small list:

originContent: - `--debug=`: Options are `console` and `none`. If `console`, will redirect kernel debug output to the screen. Useful for debugging
translatedContent: - `--debug=`: Options are `console` and `none`. If `console`, will redirect kernel debug output to the screen. Useful for debugging

originContent: - `--noload=`: Comma-separated list of driver (.sys) files to not load. Problematic drivers: usb_xhci.sys, ahci.sys, ps2.sys (if you don't support PS/2),
translatedContent: - `--noload=`: Comma-separated list of driver (.sys) files to not load. Problematic drivers: usb_xhci.sys, ahci.sys, ps2.sys (if you don't support PS/2),

originContent: - `--no-acpica`: Disable the ACPICA library and fallback to MinACPI implementation. Only useful in extreme cases.
translatedContent: - `--no-acpica`: Disable the ACPICA library and fallback to MinACPI implementation. Only useful in extreme cases.

originContent: - `--no-acpi`: Disable all ACPI implementations. Disables SMP as well.
translatedContent: - `--no-acpi`: Disable all ACPI implementations. Disables SMP as well.

originContent: - `--disable-smp`: Enable ACPI, but disable SMP
translatedContent: - `--disable-smp`: Enable ACPI, but disable SMP

originContent: - `--disable-cow`: Disable copy-on-write. Not recommended, but can be useful in extreme cases.
translatedContent: - `--disable-cow`: Disable copy-on-write. Not recommended, but can be useful in extreme cases.

originContent: - `--no-psf-font`: Don't load the PSF font from initrd
translatedContent: - `--no-psf-font`: Don't load the PSF font from initrd

originContent: ## External components
translatedContent: ## External components

originContent: Certain external components are available in `external`, `libc`, and other parts of the kernel. Here is a list of them and their versions:
translatedContent: Certain external components are available in `external`, `libc`, and other parts of the kernel. Here is a list of them and their versions:

originContent: - ACPICA UNIX* (Intel License): Version 20240927 [available here](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/topic-technology/open/acpica/download.html)
translatedContent: - ACPICA UNIX* (Intel License): Version 20240927 [available here](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/topic-technology/open/acpica/download.html)

originContent: - libmusl math library (MIT License): [available here](https://github.com/kraj/musl)
translatedContent: - libmusl math library (MIT License): [available here](https://github.com/kraj/musl)

originContent: - freetype (GPL license): [available here](https://sourceforge.net/projects/freetype/)
translatedContent: - freetype (GPL license): [available here](https://sourceforge.net/projects/freetype/)

originContent: - mlibc (MIT license): [Ethereal fork available here](https://github.com/sasdallas/mlibc)
translatedContent: - mlibc (MIT license): [Ethereal fork available here](https://github.com/sasdallas/mlibc)

originContent: - tinf (zlib license): [available here](https://github.com/jibsen/tinf/)
translatedContent: - tinf (zlib license): [available here](https://github.com/jibsen/tinf/)

originContent: - json-parser (BSD 2-clause license): [available here](https://github.com/json-parser/json-parser)
translatedContent: - json-parser (BSD 2-clause license): [available here](https://github.com/json-parser/json-parser)

originContent: - PlutoSVG (MIT license): [available here](https://github.com/sammycage/plutosvg)
translatedContent: - PlutoSVG (MIT license): [available here](https://github.com/sammycage/plutosvg)

originContent: ## Credits
translatedContent: ## Credits

originContent: A lot of Ethereal's design was inspired by [ToaruOS by klange](https://github.com/klange/ToaruOS) - thank you!
translatedContent: A lot of Ethereal's design was inspired by [ToaruOS by klange](https://github.com/klange/ToaruOS) - thank you!

originContent: Ethereal's virtual memory manager design and other parts was inspired by [Astral by @mathewnd](https://github.com/mathewnd/Astral)
translatedContent: Ethereal's virtual memory manager design and other parts was inspired by [Astral by @mathewnd](https://github.com/mathewnd/Astral)

originContent: Some code from Astral was also used, credited where appropriate. A license file is included in `external/`.
translatedContent: Some code from Astral was also used, credited where appropriate. A license file is included in `external/`.

originContent: The Ethereal logo and Mercury theme were designed by the artist [ArtsySquid](https://artsycomms.carrd.co)
translatedContent: The Ethereal logo and Mercury theme were designed by the artist [ArtsySquid](https://artsycomms.carrd.co)

originContent: ## Licensing
translatedContent: ## Licensing

originContent: Hexahedron and all other non-external components of Ethereal fall under the terms of the BSD 3-clause license (available in LICENSE).\
translatedContent: Hexahedron and all other non-external components of Ethereal fall under the terms of the BSD 3-clause license (available in LICENSE).\

originContent: All files, unless specified in the copyright header, fall under this license. Any file without a copyright header is NOT protected by BSD 3-clause.
translatedContent: All files, unless specified in the copyright header, fall under this license. Any file without a copyright header is NOT protected by BSD 3-clause.

originContent: **LICENSING ISSUES:** If a file is found without proper commenting, immediately contact me (preferably through a public channel such as GitHub issues for transparency) directly to resolve it.
translatedContent: **LICENSING ISSUES:** If a file is found without proper commenting, immediately contact me (preferably through a public channel such as GitHub issues for transparency) directly to resolve it.

originContent: Ethereal's goal has **NEVER** been to take code, but it has happened in the past. Please contact me!
translatedContent: Ethereal's goal has **NEVER** been to take code, but it has happened in the past. Please contact me!
